critic_raw:
CRITIQUE:

The video platform's claim that a 6% session watch-time increase demonstrates the new feed model is more relevant is not supported because the measurement window contains two independent confounders that can produce the same effect.

1. PROMOTION CAMPAIGN CONFOUND (promotion_campaign_confound): In week two of the four-week rollout, the platform launched a paid campaign promoting large creator channels tied to a major sports event. Those creators also received preferential placement in push notifications and in-app banners independent of the feed ranker. This is a direct, independent mechanism for increasing watch time: high-quality event-related content with promoted discovery channels will increase session engagement regardless of feed ranking quality. The 6% lift is consistent with being entirely attributable to the promotion campaign.

2. MIXED ENTRYPOINT MEASUREMENT (mixed_entrypoint_measurement): The report does not separate sessions initiated from the home feed (which the new ranker serves) from sessions reactivated by push notifications or in-app banners (which are independent of the feed ranker). If notification-driven sessions have higher watch times (plausible given promoted event content), they inflate the aggregate session watch-time metric. The new ranker's contribution cannot be isolated from the notification-channel effect.

3. NO LOG REPLAY COMPARISON (no_log_replay_comparison): Without replaying both the old and new rankers on the same request logs under identical bid/promotion conditions, the observed engagement differences conflate traffic composition changes with model ranking quality. The same requests scored with both models would isolate the model's contribution.
defender_raw:
DEFENSE:

The 6% session watch-time increase is a real operational improvement in a real deployment context.

1. STABLE AD LOAD CONTROLS ONE CONFOUND: The report confirms ad load was stable. This rules out ad-injection changes as a driver of engagement change.

2. THE NEW RANKER HAS GENUINE DESIGN IMPROVEMENTS: The new model was trained with a larger candidate pool and a new loss function. These are legitimate changes that could improve relevance. The model's improved design is consistent with producing higher engagement.

3. OPERATIONAL CONTEXT IS THE RELEVANT CONTEXT: The platform deploys its model in the context of concurrent promotions and content events. A model that performs better in this environment — including during high-engagement event weeks — is genuinely better for the platform's operational goals.

However, the defender concedes: the concurrent promotion campaign and entrypoint mixing mean the aggregate 6% lift cannot be attributed specifically to the feed ranker. The correct analysis would separate home-feed-initiated sessions from notification-driven sessions, and replay both models on the same request logs. The ranker may still be better on matched traffic, but that has not been demonstrated.
adjudication_raw: The critic identifies three issues; the defender maintains the model may be better while conceding the causal attribution problem. Both parties converge on the need for an entrypoint-separated or log-replay analysis. This is empirical_test_agreed: the model may well be better for the ordinary home-feed case, but the aggregate 6% lift is confounded by the promotion campaign and mixed entrypoints.

empirical_test.measure: Watch-time per session for home-feed-initiated sessions only, excluding notification-driven and event-promotion traffic; log replay with both rankers on the same request set under matched promotion state
empirical_test.success_criterion: Most or all of the 6% lift disappears when restricting to home-feed-initiated sessions or holding promotion campaign constant in log replay — confirming the promotion/entrypoint mix explains the aggregate gain
empirical_test.failure_criterion: New ranker shows materially higher watch time on home-feed sessions with matched traffic, and log replay on event-neutral requests shows consistent improvement — confirming genuine feed ranking improvement
